ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general iced tea entry; no specific 'less sugar flavoured packaged iced tea' code exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific less-sugar flavoured RTD iced tea sub-code identified. Approximate match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for flavoured packaged iced tea with reduced sugar. Left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve packaged iced tea bottle in Singapore is approximately 500 ml (500 g), providing 175 kcal and 44 g of sugar per bottle.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured Iced Tea (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for flavoured iced tea (less sugar, packaged), cr_id D09020028. All core macros and secondary nutrients reflect HPB-reported values.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific less-sugar packaged flavoured iced tea. GI of 56 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for commercially sweetened iced tea (sucrose-sweetened, RTD), which is the closest approved analogous food. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (56 × 44 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 24.6. The 'less sugar' formulation may yield a slightly lower GI in practice; this estimate is conservative and should be updated if a direct measurement becomes available.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (8.8 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk; sodium is low at 3 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is negligible (3 mg/100 g), the 8.8 g/100 g sugar load in this RTD iced tea is associated with elevated blood pressure via fructose-mediated uric acid production and sympathetic nervous system activation (AHA 2018); patients with hypertension should replace this beverage with unsweetened brewed tea or plain water.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.8 g sugar/100 g and estimated GI of 56; a 500 ml serving delivers 44 g sugar and a glycemic load of ~24.6
type2_diabetes.guidance: This packaged iced tea is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) with 8.8 g sugar/100 g and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly, causing acute postprandial glucose spikes, and regular SSB consumption is independently associated with increased type 2 diabetes incidence — individuals with or at risk of type 2 diabetes should avoid this product and choose unsweetened tea or water instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular SSB consumption linked to dyslipidaemia, visceral adiposity, and elevated triglycerides; no protective nutrients present
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Habitual consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as this flavoured iced tea is associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and increased cardiovascular disease risk (AHA/ACC 2019); the absence of fibre, omega-3 fatty acids, or any cardioprotective nutrient means this product offers no cardiovascular benefit and should be consumed only occasionally if at all.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured Iced Tea (Less Sugar, Packaged) is a commercially produced, ready-to-drink (RTD) cold tea beverage sold in sealed bottles or cans in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is made from brewed or extracted tea (typically black or green tea base) combined with water, added flavourings (such as lemon, peach, or jasmine), sugar, and acidity regulators. The 'less sugar' designation indicates a reduced sugar formulation compared to standard iced tea products, but the product still contains 8.8 g of sugar per 100 ml — equivalent to 44 g of sugar in a typical 500 ml bottle. The beverage is clear to lightly amber in colour, mildly sweet, and slightly tangy depending on the flavour variant. It is served chilled and is widely consumed as a refreshment during meals, on-the-go, or in warm weather. Despite the 'less sugar' label, this product is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under WHO, HPB Singapore, and AHA guidelines because its sugar content exceeds the 5 g/100 ml threshold. Clinically, it poses a high concern for individuals with type 2 diabetes due to rapid glucose absorption from liquid sugar with no fibre buffering, a moderate concern for hypertension due to fructose-mediated blood pressure elevation, and a moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to SSB-associated dyslipidaemia. It provides 35 kcal per 100 ml, with all energy derived from carbohydrates (8.8 g/100 ml). Protein and fat are absent by product formulation. Sodium is very low at 3 mg/100 ml. This product is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Unsweetened brewed tea is a strongly preferred alternative for health-conscious consumers.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020028) with confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients are exact matches from the approved primary regional source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, which slightly reduces overall confidence for the GI component only; all other fields are high-confidence.
notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is confirmed zero by HPB source — this is a water-and-sugar-based beverage containing no protein by formulation. fat_g is confirmed zero by HPB source — no fat-containing ingredients are present in this RTD tea product. saturated_fat_g and dietary_fibre_g are also confirmed zero per HPB data. Negative sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 because detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this specific packaged RTD iced tea product are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. calcium_mg and iron_mg are set to 0.0 per HPB-reported values. GI note: GI of 56 is an analogous estimate based on commercially sweetened RTD iced tea from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific less-sugar formulation was found in approved sources. Glycemic load calculated for a 500 g serving: (56 × 44) / 100 = 24.6. Multilingual note: This product is commercially sold in Singapore and the multilingual names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been added to satisfy G6 and Q1/Q8 requirements for Singapore-market products. local_names and aliases_by_language have been fully populated with Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi forms. Ontology note: FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are approximate matches; no exact ontology code for 'less sugar flavoured packaged iced tea' was identified in FoodOn or FoodEx2 as of 2026.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'.

Iteration 3: Added mandatory multilingual local_names for Chinese simplified (调味冰茶（少糖，瓶装）), Chinese traditional (調味冰茶（少糖，瓶裝）), Malay (Teh Ais Berperisa (Kurang Gula, Bungkusan)), Tamil (சுவையூட்டப்பட்ட ஐஸ் தேநீர் (குறைந்த சர்க்கரை, பேக்கேஜ்)), and Hindi (स्वादयुक्त आइस्ड टी (कम चीनी, पैकेज्ड)) to satisfy G6 hard gate. Added all multilingual strings to flat aliases array and aliases_by_language to satisfy Q1 and Q8.
